claims.claim: Using a synthetic regression task with a softmax-routed 12-expert MoE, we compare four recovery paths across 48 random seeds: prune-only recovery (6 experts), prune-then-upcycle (saliency-selected 6 duplicated and jittered back to 12), random-survivor upcycle, and vanilla unpruned expert diversification.

claims.claim: We define the **prune-then-upcycle (PTU)** loop as: (1) compute saliency scores for all experts, (2) prune low-saliency experts, (3) duplicate surviving experts with small perturbation ("jitter") back to the original expert count, and (4) recover via continued training.

evidence_refs.quote: We investigate whether pruning weak experts from a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model and then upcycling the surviving experts back to the original expert count can yield better recovery-after-compression than prune-only baselines.
